body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: partial

## Reasoning

### agent_supports: yes

Aider supports a non-interactive, single-shot turn via the `--message`/`-m` flag: it processes one natural-language instruction, applies any edits, and exits â€” no interactive chat session. See https://aider.chat/docs/scripting.html. This is exactly the `headless_mode` capability the `basic-turn` primitive requires (`replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json` â†’ `headless_mode: true`).

The `basic-turn` recipe prompt â€” "Reply with exactly the word: ok" â€” is a pure one-message round-trip with no tool use (`tool_calls_max: 0`), which is the smallest turn aider can run. Aider persists every exchange to `.aider.chat.history.md` in the project CWD (https://aider.chat/docs/faq.html) and prints a `> Tokens: X sent, Y received.` line after each model call (e.g. `Tokens: 7.7k sent, 167 received`), confirmed across the aider docs and HISTORY. So a clean single turn produces a fully-formed transcript with real token usage.

### irrlicht_observes: partial

The aider adapter is `FilesUnderCWD{Filename: ".aider.chat.history.md", Parser: RawLineParser}` (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/aider/agent.go`). Walking the three User-observable signals against the parser as it exists today:

1. **`ready â†’ working`** â€” yes. The `#### ` user-prompt line emits a `user_message` event (`parser.go` `ParseLineRaw`). The SessionDetector force-bounces a ready session to working when fresh activity arrives with a non-empty `LastEventType` (`session_detector_activity.go:343-350`, `ForceReadyToWorkingReason`), so the working state is entered.
2. **`TotalTokens > 0`** â€” yes. The `> Tokens:` line is matched by `tokensRE` and routed through `closeModelCall`, which emits an `assistant_message` carrying a `TokenSnapshot{Input, Output, Total}`. `applyTokenSnapshot` (`tailer_metrics.go:119-125`) sets `metrics.TotalTokens = tokens.Total` whenever `Total > 0`. For the recipe's `> Tokens: 771 sent, 1 received.` this yields `TotalTokens = 772`. Signal fully met.
3. **`working â†’ ready`** â€” yes, but DELAYED. Aider's idle TUI prompt never lands in the markdown history, so there is no in-band turn-end marker. The parser synthesizes `turn_done` out-of-band via `IdleFlush` once the transcript has been quiet for `aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter` (1500ms). `turn_done` is the authoritative end-of-turn signal (`session.go:478-481` `IsAgentDone`), flipping the session back to ready. The state outcome is CORRECT â€” the final state is `ready` and the topology is `ready â†’ working â†’ ready`.

The carve-out that keeps this at `partial` rather than `yes` is **latency**, not correctness. The 1500ms idle floor is only a floor: the daemon's SessionDetector re-polls working sessions every `staleWorkingRefreshInterval` (5s), so the effective workingâ†’ready delay after the last transcript line is roughly `max(1500ms, next 5s poll tick)` â‰ˆ 5s in practice (documented at `parser.go:42-54`). The session sits in `working` for several extra seconds after aider has actually finished. The arc and token count are observed accurately; the workingâ†’ready edge is just late.
caveats: feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant: aider's idle TUI prompt is never written to .aider.chat.history.md, so there is no in-band turn-end marker. The parser synthesizes turn_done via IdleFlush instead â€” the state arc is correct, but the end-of-turn is inferred from quiescence rather than observed.

metric-drift-downstream: the workingâ†’ready transition lands with ~5s effective latency (1500ms IdleFlush floor clamped to the SessionDetector's 5s working-refresh poll), so the session shows as working for several seconds after aider has finished. State correctness is unaffected; only the transition timing is delayed.

sources.note: --message/-m one-shot non-interactive mode: process one instruction, apply edits, exit
aider writes chat history to .aider.chat.history.md in the project directory
aider prints '> Tokens: X sent, Y received.' per model call; total tokens sent/received tracked
Source = FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser} â€” per-project markdown transcript

#### â†’ user_message; > Tokens: â†’ assistant_message w/ TokenSnapshot; no in-band turn-end â†’ IdleFlush synthesizes turn_done (1500ms floor); ~5s effective workingâ†’ready latency documented at lines 42-54
applyTokenSnapshot (lines 119-125): metrics.TotalTokens = tokens.Total when Total > 0 â€” drives the TotalTokens>0 signal
IsAgentDone (lines 461-496): LastEventType == turn_done is the authoritative end-of-turn â†’ flips working to ready
lines 343-350: force readyâ†’working on fresh activity so ClassifyState can later detect the workingâ†’ready edge
TestParser_BaselineHello_FullTurn (lines 35-118): uses the exact basic-turn recipe prompt; pins assistant_message+TokenSnapshot on > Tokens: and turn_done from IdleFlush
headless_mode: true â€” the primitive's required capability
